steps.description: Prepare 0.2 M mixed salt solutions containing zirconium oxychloride and cerium nitrate at concentrations corresponding to 5 mol% CeO2 in ZrO2.
Co-precipitate the mixed salt solution at pH=9 using ammonia solution for pH control, while stirring.
Dry the precipitate gels.
Calcine the dried precipitate at 600°C.
Grind the calcined powder, then wet ball-mill and sieve to produce ceria-doped zirconia powder.
Add ethanolic solution of gadolinium nitrate (0.85 mol% Gd2O3) to the ceria/zirconia powder and mix.
Dry the mixed powder.
Calcine the dried mixed powder.
Grind and wet ball-mill the calcined mixed powder once again.
Press rectangular samples (35 mm × 8 mm × 2 mm) at 100 MPa. Green densities of 43% TD.
Sinter the pressed compacts in air at temperatures from 1400–1600°C for 2 h.

synthesis.notes: The gadolinium nitrate was added as an ethanolic solution to the previously co-precipitated ceria/zirconia powder (5 mol% CeO2), rather than being co-precipitated in a single step. The final composition is designated '5CeGd' (0.85 mol% Gd2O3 / 5 mol% CeO2 / ZrO2). Sintering at 1400°C gave 97.4% TD; at 1500°C gave 99.4% TD; at 1600°C gave 99.5% TD. Fine-grained ceramics with ~2 μm average grain size were obtained at 1500°C.

steps.description: Prepare 0.2 M mixed salt solutions containing zirconium oxychloride and cerium nitrate with corresponding concentrations for 5 and 10 mol % CeO2
Coprecipitation at pH=9 using ammonia solution for control while stirring
Dry the precipitate gels
Calcine the dried precipitate gels
Grind, wet ball-mill and sieve to produce ceria-doped zirconia powders

Add ethanolic solutions of gadolinium nitrate with different concentrations to produce zirconia powders containing 0.85 mol % gadolinia/5 mol % ceria (5CeGd) and 0.5 mol % gadolinia/10 mol % ceria (10CeGd)
Dry the mixed powders
Calcine the dried mixed powders
Grind and wet ball-mill the calcined powders
Press rectangular samples (35 mm length, 8 mm width, 2 mm thick) at 100 MPa
Sinter the samples in air

steps.description: Prepare 0.2 M mixed salt solutions containing the corresponding concentrations of zirconium oxychloride and cerium nitrate.
Co-precipitate at pH=9 using ammonia solution for control, while stirring.
Dry the precipitate gels.
Calcined at 600 °C.
Ground, wet ball-milled followed by sieving to produce ceria-doped zirconia powders.
Add ethanolic solutions of gadolinium nitrate with different concentrations, and mix with each of the above powders to produce zirconia powders containing 0.85 mol % gadolinia/5 mol % ceria.
The mixed powders were then dried.
Calcined.
Ground and finally wet ball-milled once again.

Calcinate again.
Grind and wet ball-mill once again.

steps.description: Prepare 0.2 M mixed salt solutions containing the corresponding concentrations of zirconium oxychloride and cerium nitrate (10 mol% CeO2).
Co-precipitate at pH=9 using ammonia solution for control, while stirring.
Dry the precipitate gels.
Calcinate at 600 °C.
Grind, wet ball-mill followed by sieving to produce ceria-doped zirconia powders.
Add and mix an ethanolic solution of gadolinium nitrate (to produce 0.5 mol % gadolinia) with the ceria-doped zirconia powder.
Dry the mixed powders.
Calcinate the dried powders.
Grind and wet ball-mill once again.
